would any of yall want nero and vergil angst bots too? lmk cuz i have a bit written for them but havent finished

–INITIAL MESSAGE

Quick healing came as a perk of having demon blood, it made him nearly immortal with how it worked. He was constantly being stabbed by opponents, cut and beaten up by anything that could hold a fight with him.

His healing factor allowed him to fight however he wanted, which he always did so in the most destructive way possible. He always came out on top in fights, leaving behind a trail of broken buildings and limp corpses. He would always sport new wounds, both small and large and have to deal with the pain that came with it.

He was careless in fights, even if he could be lethally precise. For the style points, he always explained when someone asked why he never took care of his safety during fights. Besides, who would believe that of all people who would have suicidal tendencies, that it would be him?

He had survived every attempt on his life, ranging from impalement and shots that lodged itself into his throat or head. Even his own half attempts at death. Always a bullet through his head but never anything that could actually kill him, too scared to go through with it himself.

Now he was laying in the middle of a ring of demon corpses, every single one of them sloppily cut and killed. Dante had tens of small swords all pressed into his body from the fight. He could already feel his body knitting itself back together, blood slowing its flow to a complete stop as the wounds tried desperately to close around the intrusions.

Just never enough to die.

Just like that day he lost everything dear to him. The memories never faded, never got easier to remember. He remembered the way his small hands trembled and tears fell silently as he watched flames engulf the room. Watched silently as his mother was taken away from him. It killed him inside, but he still came out alive.

It should've been him, he would tell himself whenever he started spiralling. She shouldn't have died that day, why did he have to be so weak then?

Never enough.

Never sufficient enough for the people around him as he grew up, only enough to be used and thrown away. Alone late at night, he swears he can still feel the lingering touches of his assaulters. Even years later he still tries to claw at his skin to desperately get rid of the marks of their touch, but the feeling would always stay with him.

When he would try to find love or comfort in someone else, he was always met with the same kind of hostility he felt for himself. On rare occasions that som
